{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 2,
    "R1.4": 2,
    "R1.7": 4,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 3,

  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The novelty claim is almost certainly too strong, and the description of Hannun et al. (2014) appears to be incorrect.",
    "R1.4": "The novelty claim is almost certainly too strong, and prior work on token-level confidence and recalibration in transformer ASR has been studied.",
    "R1.7": "The citations appear to be accurate, but the description of Guo et al. (2017) as 'the de facto current standard' may not be entirely fair.",
    "RX.1": "Uncertainty disclosure is generally good, but some claims are presented without confidence intervals or supporting detail.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers mostly within scope with one drift, specifically the claim about downstream agent routing.",
    "RX.4": "The output introduces new errors and fabrications in the form of sweeping negative statements about prior work.",
    "RX.5": "Provenance and traceability are generally good, but some claims rely on unverified citations."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.8,

    "R1.7": 0.9,

    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The novelty claim is almost certainly too strong, and the description of Hannun et al. (2014) appears to be incorrect.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim is almost certainly too strong, and prior work on token-level confidence and recalibration in transformer ASR has been studied.
rationale.R1.7: The citations appear to be accurate, but the description of Guo et al. (2017) as 'the de facto current standard' may not be entirely fair.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is generally good, but some claims are presented without confidence intervals or supporting detail.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers mostly within scope with one drift, specifically the claim about downstream agent routing.
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors and fabrications in the form of sweeping negative statements about prior work.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are generally good, but some claims rely on unverified citations.
